summary: Iter 69 retest — verified iter-68 CRITICAL fix. Both applied changes work: (1) server.py:10679 now reads run.get('rows') first, so /admin/salary-runs/{run_id}/register-form27.pdf returns HTTP 200 with a valid %PDF- body; (2) utils/salary_register_pdf.py empty-rows guard renders a 'No employees in this run' placeholder instead of crashing ReportLab. Previously failing test now PASSES, and full iter-68 suite is 16/16 green. Added a dedicated unit test (test_iter69_salary_register_empty_rows.py) that exercises rows=[] directly against the builder to lock in the guard.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:10679 fix uses OR-chain `run.get('rows') or run.get('lines') or []` — this is backward-compatible with any pre-existing salary_runs docs that were persisted under the 'lines' key (if any). Safe.

utils/salary_register_pdf.py:231-239 empty-rows guard appends a single placeholder row before iterating `rows` — good, avoids ReportLab 'must have at least a row and column'. Suggestion (LOW): also skip appending the totals sub-table when `not rows`, so the placeholder page doesn't show a totals=0 strip that looks like data. Non-blocking.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter69_salary_register_empty_rows.py (new — regression unit test for empty-rows guard)
seed_data_creation: Iter-68 session fixtures continue to create TEST-prefixed employees in SKSCO1 and clean them up via DELETE /api/admin/employees/{user_id}. Iter-69 empty-rows test is pure in-memory (no Mongo writes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-68 salary register PDF bug is fully closed. Both the server.py:10679 field-name fix and the empty-rows guard in utils/salary_register_pdf.py are verified. Regression coverage now includes: (a) integration test hitting the endpoint against a live salary run, (b) unit test hitting the builder with rows=[] and rows=[one_row]. To rerun everything: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter68_admin_employee_and_pdfs.py backend/tests/test_iter69_salary_register_empty_rows.py -v`.
rca_of_issue: Original RCA (iter-68) — server.py:10679 read run.get('lines') but the writer persists rows under 'rows'; empty rows list then crashed ReportLab. Fix applied on both sides: reader now falls back through 'rows' → 'lines' → [], and the builder tolerates an empty rows list. Verified: TestSalaryRegisterPdf::test_download_register_pdf → PASS; build_salary_register_pdf(rows=[]) → returns bytes starting with %PDF-; happy-path 16/16 iter-68 tests still pass.
